input.prompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

input.initialPrompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

data.content:
Here's a complete example:

**XAML**
```xml
<RefreshView Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}"
             IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}">
    <CollectionView ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
                    SelectionMode="Single"
                    SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem}"
                    SelectionChangedCommand="{Binding SelectionChangedCommand}">
        <CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
            <DataTemplate x:DataType="models:Item">
                <Grid Padding="12" ColumnDefinitions="*,Auto">
                    <Label Text="{Binding Name}" FontSize="16" />
                    <Label Grid.Column="1" Text="{Binding Status}"
                           TextColor="Gray" />
                </Grid>
            </DataTemplate>
        </CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
    </CollectionView>
</RefreshView>
```

**ViewModel** (using CommunityToolkit.Mvvm)
```csharp
public partial class ItemsViewModel : ObservableObject
{
    private readonly IApiService _api;
    private readonly INavigationService _nav;

    [ObservableProperty] ObservableCollection<Item> items = [];
    [ObservableProperty] bool isRefreshing;
    [ObservableProperty] Item? selectedItem;

    [RelayCommand]
    async Task Refresh()
    {
        Items = new(await _api.GetItemsAsync());
        IsRefreshing = false;           // stops the spinner
    }

    [RelayCommand]
    async Task SelectionChanged()
    {
        if (SelectedItem is null) return;
        await _nav.GoToAsync(nameof(DetailPage),
            new Dictionary<string, object> { ["Item"] = SelectedItem });
        SelectedItem = null;            // reset so the same row is re-selectable
    }
}
```

**Key binding points:**

| XAML Property | ViewModel Property | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `IsRefreshing` | `IsRefreshing` | Two-way; set `false` to stop spinner |
| `Command` (RefreshView) | `RefreshCommand` | Triggers data reload |
| `ItemsSource` | `Items` | The collection |
| `SelectedItem` | `SelectedItem` | Two-way; drives navigation |
| `SelectionChangedCommand` | `SelectionChangedCommand` | Fires on tap |

**Important details:**
- Set `SelectedItem = null` after navigating so the user can re-select the same row.
- Set `IsRefreshing = false` at the end of `Refresh()` — the `RefreshView` won't dismiss automatically.
- `SelectionMode="Single"` limits selection to one item; use `None` to disable or `Multiple` for multi-select.
